channel.title: gsoc21 on Rohit Goswami
channel.description: Recent content in gsoc21 on Rohit Goswami

item.title: GSoC21 W8: LFortran Refactors, and Compile Time Evaluations
item.description: Language standardese and implementations Background Serialized update for the 2021 Google Summer of Code under the fortran-lang organization, mentored by Ondrej Certik. Series This post is part of a series based around my weekly GSoC21 project check-ins. GSoC21 W1: LFortran Kickoff GSoC21 W2: LFortran Unraveling GSoC21 W3: Kind, Characters, and Standards GSoC21 W4: LFortran, Backends and Bugs GSoC21 W5: LFortran Design Details and minidftatom GSoC21 W6: LFortran ASR and Values GSoC21 W7: LFortran Workflow Basics GSoC21 W8: LFortran Refactors, and Compile Time Evaluations <– You are here!

item.title: GSoC21 W7: LFortran Workflow Basics
item.description: Revisiting minidftatom and a birds eye view of lfortran Background Serialized update for the 2021 Google Summer of Code under the fortran-lang organization, mentored by Ondrej Certik. Series This post is part of a series based around my weekly GSoC21 project check-ins. GSoC21 W1: LFortran Kickoff GSoC21 W2: LFortran Unraveling GSoC21 W3: Kind, Characters, and Standards GSoC21 W4: LFortran, Backends and Bugs GSoC21 W5: LFortran Design Details and minidftatom GSoC21 W6: LFortran ASR and Values GSoC21 W7: LFortran Workflow Basics <– You are here!

GSoC21 W1: LFortran Kickoff GSoC21 W2: LFortran Unraveling GSoC21 W3: Kind, Characters, and Standards GSoC21 W4: LFortran, Backends and Bugs GSoC21 W5: LFortran Design Details and minidftatom <– You are here!

Series This post is part of a series based around my weekly GSoC21 project check-ins. GSoC21 W1: LFortran Kickoff GSoC21 W2: LFortran Unraveling GSoC21 W3: Kind, Characters, and Standards GSoC21 W4: LFortran, Backends and Bugs <– You are here!

GSoC21 W1: LFortran Kickoff GSoC21 W2: LFortran Unraveling GSoC21 W3: Kind, Characters, and Standards <– You are here! GSoC21 W4: LFortran, Backends and Bugs

item.title: GSoC21 W2: LFortran Unraveling
item.description: Delving into language standards and back-ends for lfortran Background As discussed in a previous post in this series, I have been spending roughly half of each working day with LFortran as part of the 2021 Google Summer of Code under the fortran-lang organization, mentored by Ondrej Certik. Series This post is part of a series based around my weekly GSoC21 project check-ins. GSoC21 W1: LFortran Kickoff

item.title: GSoC21 W1: LFortran Kickoff
item.description: Charting paths towards concrete lfortran usage Background As mentioned in an earlier post, I have had the immense pleasure of continuing development of the disruptive lfortran compiler under the aegis of the Fortran Lang organization, financed by the Google Summer of Code and mentored ably by Ondrej Certik. A rather interesting consequence of this is that we are strongly encouraged to write a precis of our activities each week.
